perturbation.original_text: For calendar year 2016, the Franchise Fee shall be paid to Grantor on or before March 31, 2017.
perturbation.changed_text: For calendar year 2016, the Franchise Fee shall be paid to Grantor on or before June 30, 2017.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in payment terms. Initially, the deadline for the 2016 Franchise Fee payment was March 31, 2017. Changing this deadline to June 30, 2017, contradicts the clause, creating uncertainty about the actual due date. This affects the enforcement of late payment penalties, which might be tied to the original March 31 deadline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For each succeeding calendar year during the term of this Franchise, the total annual Franchise Fee paid by Grantee to the localities shall be the base year total annual Franchise Fee increased by three (3) percent compounded annually over the term of the Franchise. For each calendar year during the term of this Franchise, Grantor's percentage share shall be determined pursuant to this Section, and paid to Grantor on or before March 31 of the succeeding calendar year.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a payment deadline of March 31, 2017, for the 2016 Franchise Fee. The changed text alters this deadline to June 30, 2017. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that the Grantor's percentage share shall be 'paid to Grantor on or before March 31 of the succeeding calendar year.' Since the change modifies the due date, it creates a clear inconsistency regarding when the payment should be made, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All notices required under this Agreement or the Ordinance shall be in writing and shall be deemed validly given, unless otherwise required, when sent by certified mail, return receipt requested, or by a nationally recognized overnight courier, addressed as follows (or any other address the party to be notified may have designated to the sender by like notice):
perturbation.changed_text: All notices required under this Agreement or the Ordinance shall be in writing and shall be deemed validly given, unless otherwise required, when sent by certified mail, return receipt requested, or by email, addressed as follows (or any other address the party to be notified may have designated to the sender by like notice):
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'nationally recognized overnight courier' to 'email' as an acceptable method of delivering notices, we introduce a potential contradiction. Email delivery lacks the guaranteed receipt and tracking of a courier or certified mail. The original intention of 'return receipt requested' implies a need for verifiable delivery, which email doesn't inherently provide, creating uncertainty about what constitutes valid notice. This could affect clauses that depend on timely notification, creating disputes over whether notice was properly given.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices required under this Agreement or the Ordinance shall be in writing and shall be deemed validly given, unless otherwise required, when sent by certified mail, return receipt requested, or by email, addressed as follows (or any other address the party to be notified may have designated to the sender by like notice): Grantor: Grantee: Town of Vinton Roanoke Gas Company Attention: Town Manager Attention: President 311 S. Pollard Street 519 Kimball Avenue, N.E. Vinton, Virginia 24179 P.O. Box 13007 Roanoke, Virginia 24030 The parties may, by notice given under this Section, designate such other addresses as they may deem appropriate for the receipt of notices under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces email as a valid method for delivering notices, while the original text implies the need for verifiable delivery (return receipt requested). Email lacks the inherent guaranteed receipt and tracking of certified mail or a courier, creating a contradiction in how valid notice is established, contradicting the intended function of 'return receipt requested'. This directly impacts the enforceability of clauses dependent on timely and verifiable notification. The changed text and contradicted text are actually at the same location.
